title: Evangelion 3333 Bluray coming out on 8 25
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 3.333 Bluray coming out on 8/25             Quote Post by xenosaga » Sat Jun 19, 2021 6:44 am tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com')) The bluray version IMAX special version they was shown in January is coming out on August 25th. They are also having special 3.333 -> Shin screening event on June 27th with another stage greeting event with the cast.

[]: Re: Evangelion 3.333 Bluray coming out on 8/25             Quote Post by FullMetalBiscuit » Sat Jun 19, 2021 7:53 am I got very excited for a second and then saw that it wasn't 3.0+1.0. Still, glad we're getting that outside of cinema. Now it would be wise to assume that 3.0+1.0 wont come until after that date, which is still within expectations.

[]: Re: Evangelion 3.333 Bluray coming out on 8/25             Quote Post by Hooded_Omar » Sat Jun 19, 2021 8:07 am Since 3.333 is just visual changes as far as I know, will we get a dub version too since they can just use the same audio track from 3.33?

[]: Re: Evangelion 3.333 Bluray coming out on 8/25             Quote Post by dzzthink » Sat Jun 19, 2021 8:15 am Whenever I buy Blu-rays, there's always this expectation of new material and special features, but this is rarely the case for most movies. Evangelion 3.333 might be an exception but I just don't feel there is any novelty in owning Blu-rays copies anymore, except that they are more expensive DVDs.

[]: Re: Evangelion 3.333 Bluray coming out on 8/25             Quote Post by xenosaga » Sat Jun 19, 2021 8:16 am Hooded_Omar wrote: Since 3.333 is just visual changes as far as I know, will we get a dub version too since they can just use the same audio track from 3.33?

[]: Re: Evangelion 3.333 Bluray coming out on 8/25             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sat Jun 19, 2021 9:01 am Puts on Detective Hat : August 25th is a Wednesday. The next Wednesday is September 1st. September 1st is the day the first Rebuild film, Evangelion 1.0: You Are (Not) Alone , was released in theaters all the way back in 2007. This September will mark the 14th anniversary since the new film series began. 14 is a very important number to this franchise. Therefore, Evangelion 3.0+1.0: Thrice Upon A Time will be released on Blu-ray on September 1, 2021. Case closed. Takes off Detective Hat. Sticker inside says it's actually a Conspiracy Hat. Jokes/conspiracies aside, it's exciting this is getting a release and equally exciting is a 4K Disc version is being released! Just pre-ordered mine on CDJapan!

[]: Re: Evangelion 3.333 Bluray coming out on 8/25             Quote Post by svenge » Sat Jun 19, 2021 9:13 am While I've made it a point to own at least one copy of each content revision of every Evangelion main title, I just can't get too enthused about this new 3.333 release. I don't have a UHD player, and beyond that I really don't want to pay ~$60 for the regular Blu-ray version since it doesn't have any new scenes. I guess I might pick it up used in a few years once its pricing on the secondary drops by at least half, or else there's always the possibility of it being licensed in the US by whoever gets the rights for a 1.11 re-release (GKIDS, perhaps) at which point I would be willing to pony up ~$25 for it.

[]: Re: Evangelion 3.333 Bluray coming out on 8/25             Quote Post by Hooded_Omar » Sat Jun 19, 2021 9:45 am One thing I'm curious about is the aspect ratio of 3.333. I'm not certain, but it seems that it's 16:9 instead of 21:9, judging by a cam recording of the new Shin preview included in the film. But is it expanded to 16:9 or simply cropped from 21:9?

[]: Re: Evangelion 3.333 Bluray coming out on 8/25             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Sat Jun 19, 2021 10:10 am I'd imagine that by the time we actually get our hands on this release, we'll at least know the release date for Shin's home video. EDIT: In case you're trigger happy, 3.333 is already up for preorder on CDJapan.

[]: Re: Evangelion 3.333 Bluray coming out on 8/25             Quote Post by JupiterCallisto » Sat Jun 19, 2021 10:40 am Did they at least update the NEP at the end of 3.333 with new footage? Edit : https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BdkFQ0A8hic They did

[]: Re: Evangelion 3.333 Bluray coming out on 8/25             Quote Post by svenge » Sat Jun 19, 2021 10:41 am Hooded_Omar wrote: One thing I'm curious about is the aspect ratio of 3.333. I'm not certain, but it seems that it's 16:9 instead of 21:9, judging by a cam recording of the new Shin preview included in the film. But is it expanded to 16:9 or simply cropped from 21:9?

[]: Re: Evangelion 3.333 Bluray coming out on 8/25             Quote Post by FullMetalBiscuit » Sat Jun 19, 2021 10:42 am TheFriskyIan wrote: EDIT: In case you're trigger happy, 3.333 is already up for preorder on CDJapan.

[]: Re: Evangelion 3.333 Bluray coming out on 8/25             Quote Post by svenge » Sat Jun 19, 2021 10:46 am FullMetalBiscuit wrote: TheFriskyIan#925066 wrote: EDIT: In case you're trigger happy, 3.333 is already up for preorder on CDJapan.

[]: Re: Evangelion 3.333 Bluray coming out on 8/25             Quote Post by FullMetalBiscuit » Sat Jun 19, 2021 11:10 am svenge wrote: Of course seeing that you're in Scotland, you'd have to own a region-free player to make the HK BD a viable option as European players are coded for Region "B".

[]: Re: Evangelion 3.333 Bluray coming out on 8/25             Quote Post by mastafishere » Sat Jun 19, 2021 12:52 pm Hopefully the Hong Kong release will include the 4K UHD. If not, I might import the Japanese 4K UHD, even without the English subs.

[]: Re: Evangelion 3.333 Bluray coming out on 8/25             Quote Post by svenge » Sat Jun 19, 2021 2:04 pm mastafishere wrote: Hopefully the Hong Kong release will include the 4K UHD. If not, I might import the Japanese 4K UHD, even without the English subs.

[]: Re: Evangelion 3.333 Bluray coming out on 8/25             Quote Post by HARVEST » Sun Jun 20, 2021 8:15 pm It is mentioned that the film was remastered in a 2048x858p resolution (2K CinemaScope) so the aspect ratio was not expanded to 1.90:1, 16:9 or 4:3 for IMAX for 3.333 and 3.0+1.0. Such a shame since Shin Evangelion (if it ever comes out here in IMAX) was the IMAX film I really wanted to see alongside Top Gun: Maverick, since the latter was shot in IMAX and fully expanded to 1.90:1 in IMAX. I would be even more excited if it expanded to 4:3 but I am already satisfied even with 1.90:1. Yes, IMAX is sometimes 4:3, The Lion King remake was in 1.85:1 for most of the film but expanded to 4:3 for some showings in musical numbers. Christopher Nolan films also expanded to 4:3 for some scenes in IMAX while those same scenes are expanded to 16:9 on home video. Batman v. Superman expands to 4:3 for some scenes in IMAX but the film was in CinemaScope for the entire film in its original release, until the remastered version where the film shrinks horizontally but expands vertically for the IMAX scenes, having 4:3 and CinemaScope scenes in a 16:9 frame. Zack Snyder's Justice League, a film shot entirely for IMAX, was in 4:3 for the entire film. Avengers: Infinity War/Endgame was shot in IMAX and was projected in CinemaCope for regular cinemas but expanded to 1.90:1 for IMAX.

[]: Re: Evangelion 3.333 Bluray coming out on 8/25             Quote Post by hui43210 » Tue Aug 17, 2021 8:03 am https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AnLnr_50jq8 Breakdown trailer. Really looking forward to this one too.

[]: Re: Evangelion 3.333 Bluray coming out on 8/25             Quote Post by Tlalac » Fri Aug 20, 2021 1:15 am HARVEST wrote: It is mentioned that the film was remastered in a 2048x858p resolution (2K CinemaScope) so the aspect ratio was not expanded to 1.90:1, 16:9 or 4:3 for IMAX for 3.333 and 3.0+1.0.

[]: Re: Evangelion 3.333 Bluray coming out on 8/25             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Aug 23, 2021 11:08 pm Mine was delivered today, so I can take some pictures of how it looks on my 50" TV when I get home.

 a shame since Shin Evangelion (if it ever comes out here in IMAX) was the IMAX film I really wanted to see alongside Top Gun: Maverick, since the latter was shot in IMAX and fully expanded to 1.90:1 in IMAX. I would be even more excited if it expanded to 4:3 but I am already satisfied even with 1.90:1. Yes, IMAX is sometimes 4:3, The Lion King remake was in 1.85:1 for most of the film but expanded to 4:3 for some showings in musical numbers. Christopher Nolan films also expanded to 4:3 for some scenes in IMAX while those same scenes are expanded to 16:9 on home video. Batman v. Superman expands to 4:3 for some scenes in IMAX but the film was in CinemaScope for the entire film in its original release, until the remastered version where the film shrinks horizontally but expands vertically for the IMAX scenes, having 4:3 and CinemaScope scenes in a 16:9 frame. Zack Snyder's Justice League, a film shot entirely for IMAX, was in 4:3 for the entire film. Avengers: Infinity War/Endgame was shot in IMAX and was projected in CinemaCope for regular cinemas but expanded to 1.90:1 for IMAX.
